title: Schrankia angustisiliqua
intro: Schrankia angustisiliqua is a plant species that belongs to the Fabaceae family, commonly known as a legume or bean plant. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, potentially aiding in the management of conditions such as arthritis and cardiovascular disease. The bioactive constituents of Schrankia angustisiliqua include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Schrankia angustisiliqua is a species of plant in the family Fabaceae. It is commonly known as the narrow silique moth vine. It is a perennial climber that produces small, white flowers and narrow seed pods.
